source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A semi-circle of radius 8 cm, rocks back and forth along a line. The distance between the line on which the semi-circle sits and the line above is 12 cm. As it rocks without slipping, the semi-circle touches the line above at two points. (When the semi-circle hits the line above, it immediately rocks back in the other direction.) What is the distance between these two points, in millimetres, rounded off to the nearest whole number? <image1> (Note: After finding the exact value of the desired distance, you may find a calculator useful to round this value off to the nearest whole number.)
extraction_notes: Question text contains inline placeholder <image1>; concrete image path extracted from the separate image field.
